title: Angles & Triangles
subject: Mathematics
objectives: Understand the triangle angle sum property

sections.heading: What is an angle?
sections.content:
An angle is formed when two rays share a common endpoint called the vertex. We measure angles in degrees (°). A full rotation is 360°, a straight line is 180°, and a right angle is 90°.

Angles are classified by their size:
• Acute angle: less than 90°
• Right angle: exactly 90°
• Obtuse angle: between 90° and 180°
• Straight angle: exactly 180°

sections.heading: Complementary and supplementary angles
sections.content:
Two angles are complementary if they add up to 90°. For example, 30° and 60° are complementary because 30 + 60 = 90.

Two angles are supplementary if they add up to 180°. For example, 110° and 70° are supplementary because 110 + 70 = 180.

These relationships appear constantly in geometry problems and are essential tools for finding unknown angles.

sections.heading: Finding a missing angle
sections.content:
If two angles are supplementary and one measures 135°, find the other.

Since supplementary angles sum to 180°:
  135 + x = 180
  x = 180 - 135
  x = 45°

The missing angle is 45°.

sections.heading: The triangle angle sum
sections.content:
One of the most important facts in geometry: the three interior angles of any triangle always add up to exactly 180°. This is true for every triangle — small, large, flat, tall, equilateral, or scalene.

Triangles are classified by their angles:
• Acute triangle: all angles less than 90°
• Right triangle: one angle is exactly 90°
• Obtuse triangle: one angle is greater than 90°

And by their sides:
• Equilateral: all three sides equal (all angles are 60°)
• Isosceles: two sides equal (two angles are equal)
• Scalene: no sides equal (no angles are equal)

sections.heading: Finding a missing angle in a triangle
sections.content:
A triangle has angles measuring 50° and 75°. Find the third angle.

Using the angle sum property:
  50 + 75 + x = 180
  125 + x = 180
  x = 55°

Since all angles are less than 90°, this is an acute triangle.
The interior angles of any triangle always sum to 180°. This single fact is the foundation for solving countless geometry problems.

aiContext: This lesson covers angles and triangles at a high school geometry level. Students learn angle classification, complementary/supplementary angles, the triangle angle sum theorem, and triangle classification.
